fact: Forty miles of freight tunnels run under downtown Chicago, built around 1900 to move coal and mail
source: Encyclopedia of Chicago, Chicago Historical Society / Newberry Library

fact: A city contractor driving pilings near the river cracked through a tunnel ceiling, and engineers had spotted the breach months earlier with a repair estimated at $10,000
source: Chicago Tribune contemporary reporting; also summarized in Wikipedia's 'Chicago flood' article

fact: 250 million gallons of river water surged through the tunnels into basements across downtown
source: Wikipedia, 'Chicago flood' — figure widely cited in contemporary news coverage and city reports

fact: Flooded buildings included the Board of Trade, City Hall, and Marshall Field's
source: Chicago Tribune reporting, April 1992; summarized in multiple retrospectives

fact: Total damage across the Loop was estimated at nearly two billion dollars
source: Estimates vary; commonly cited figures range from $1.5–1.95 billion. The Wikipedia article cites $1.95 billion.
